hypothesis: Incorporating explicit solvent molecule interactions through a hybrid implicit-explicit solvation scheme during ChEMBL3D dataset construction—where a small shell of discrete water molecules is included around hydrogen-bond donor/acceptor sites prior to AIMNet2 optimization—will yield conformer ensembles that better predict bioactive bound conformations in protein-ligand crystal structures, reducing median RMSD on the Platinum Diverse benchmark below 0.35 Å at ensemble size N=250 compared to the current 0.45 Å achieved with pure implicit CPCM solvation.
